votes.reason: The Python code accurately translates the Coq specifications, correctly implementing the checks for divisibility, primality, and the properties of the list of primes up to n.
The Python code correctly implements the Coq specifications for divisibility, primality, and the count_up_to_spec property, checking both directions of the iff condition and the NoDup constraint.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq specifications for divisibility, primality, and the count_up_to_spec by checking for duplicates and verifying the bidirectional implication for all relevant natural numbers.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq specifications. The divides function checks divisibility (handling d=0 correctly since 0*k=0 only when n=0). The prime function checks if p >= 2 and has no divisors in range [2, p). The count_up_to_spec function checks: (1) no duplicates via set comparison, (2) all elements in ans are primes less than n, and (3) all primes less than n are in ans. This matches the Coq biconditional specification.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq specifications. The divides function checks divisibility (handling d=0 correctly since in Coq, 0*k=0 for any k, so divides(0,n) holds iff n=0). The prime function checks p>=2 and that no d in [2,p) divides p. The count_up_to_spec function checks: (1) no duplicates via set comparison, (2) all elements in ans are primes less than n, and (3) all primes less than n are in ans. This matches the Coq biconditional specification.

The prime function checks if p >= 2 and has no divisors in range [2, p). This matches the Coq biconditional specification.
divides handles d=0 like Coq; prime checks no divisors in 2..p-1 and p>=2; count_up_to_spec enforces NoDup and that ans contains exactly all primes < n. Matches Coq on naturals.

For natural-number inputs, divides matches Coq (including d=0), prime correctly rejects any divisor in 2..p-1, and count_up_to_spec enforces NoDup and exact membership of primes < n in both directions, yielding the same concrete results.

For natural number inputs, divides matches Coq’s definition (0 divides only 0), prime checks absence of divisors 2..p-1, and count_up_to_spec enforces NoDup and exact inclusion of primes < n via both directions. The concrete behaviors align.
